title: C002: Add container image CI workflow
summary: Review of fix addressing external review #5 feedback: added `fetch-depth: 1` to the `actions/checkout` step for the upstream `openstack/${{ matrix.service }}` repository checkout. The change is a single-line addition at line 118 of `.github/workflows/build-images.yaml` that limits the clone to the tip commit at the resolved ref, avoiding hundreds of MB of unnecessary git history from long-lived OpenStack projects. A new review pattern file `.planwerk/review_patterns/require-shallow-clone-for-external-repository-checkouts-in-ci.md` was persisted to catch this in future reviews. All 75 verification tests pass. YAML validates. No regressions.

tests_checklist.item: All 75 verification tests in verify_build_images_workflow.sh pass
Edge cases covered (patch application with shallow clone is safe — git apply works on working tree)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (fetch-depth: 1 is standard GHA convention for checkouts that don't need history)
Minimal change — single line addition, no scope creep

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible (single line addition)
Compatible with downstream steps (git apply, Docker build context)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Errors detected early (existing null guards and fork PR rejection unchanged)

defensive_checklist.item: Shallow clone is safe for all downstream consumers (git apply, Dockerfile COPY)
